context.description: A philosophical passage questions human origins, uncertainty, and whether transformation beyond animal nature is possible. It contrasts genetic mutation, divine fall, and the possibility of both or neither, then asks whether there is any map out of the maze of dreams and suffering.
normalization_notes: Source language detected as German; summary translated into English.
Book title inferred from source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: Human origins are framed as either genetic mutation, a higher principle that cast humanity down, both, or neither. The passage argues that ignorance about origins need not prevent action and asks whether a map exists to lead out of the maze of dreams and beyond inner animal nature. It ends by disclaiming authority, saying the speaker does not know more than the reader.

content.free_text: book_page book_body_content theoretical_discussion Book page in German on philosophical reflection about human origins, uncertainty, and existential questions. Topics include genetic mutation, divine fall, inner animal nature, and the search for a path beyond suffering.
